sections.paragraph: to love, found once Deu 33:3. Arab. حَبَّ I. III. X., Syr. ܚܰܒ Pe. and Pa. id. The original idea is found in Breathing upon, warming, cherishing (whence חֹב the lap, the bosom, in which any thing is warmed or cherished); compare the remarks made on the root אָהַב. There is a manifest trace of this origin in the Syr. ܚܰܒ to burn, used of fire; ܚܒܒܳܐܳ a burning, heat, especially as raised by blowing. Hence, besides חֹב is derived-
